vocabulary.example_cn: 他把自己定位为政治颠覆者。

vocabulary.en: a temporary alliance of groups
vocabulary.excerpt: Building a winning coalition, tapping into all of these different many groups.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他建立了一个多元化的联盟。

vocabulary.en: a measurable factor that predicts future trends
vocabulary.excerpt: It's a leading indicator for Republican success going into November.
vocabulary.example_cn: 已婚选民投票率是领先指标。

vocabulary.en: a primary election where all candidates run together regardless of party
vocabulary.excerpt: After this primary process is complete, where this jungle primary -- let me start here -- is about survive and advance.
vocabulary.example_cn: 加州采用丛林初选制度。

vocabulary.en: the process of uniting a party's core supporters
vocabulary.excerpt: After that process is over, you're going to see base consolidation.
vocabulary.example_cn: 初选后会出现基础巩固。

vocabulary.en: a direct vote by the electorate on a specific issue
vocabulary.excerpt: The race basically fundamentally become national or a referendum on Trump?
vocabulary.example_cn: 这次选举被视为对总统的公投。

vocabulary.en: the act of turning something into a weapon
vocabulary.excerpt: President Trump's effort to create a nearly $1.8 billion anti-weaponization fund is now dead.
vocabulary.example_cn: 该基金旨在防止政府机构被武器化。

vocabulary.en: an official agreement that ends a dispute
vocabulary.excerpt: The IRS settlement which helped create the fund is still in place.
vocabulary.example_cn: 双方达成了法律和解。

vocabulary.en: a facility where people are held in custody
vocabulary.excerpt: The fight over New Jersey's Delaney Hall detention center facility now going to the state.
vocabulary.example_cn: 移民被关押在拘留中心。

vocabulary.en: without compassion for suffering
vocabulary.excerpt: Protests over alleged inhumane conditions inside are still drawing crowds outside.
vocabulary.example_cn: 抗议者谴责拘留条件不人道。

vocabulary.en: to strengthen one's commitment to a particular strategy
vocabulary.excerpt: Pelley doubled down on his criticism of CBS management.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他加倍批评公司管理层。

vocabulary.cn: 站不住脚的，难以维持的
vocabulary.en: not able to be maintained or defended
vocabulary.excerpt: The collapse of values at the top has become untenable.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他的立场变得难以维持。

vocabulary.en: the adverse results of a situation
vocabulary.excerpt: The fallout continues in Maine for Senate candidate Graham Platner.
vocabulary.example_cn: 丑闻的余波仍在持续。

vocabulary.en: a person who supports a particular cause or group
vocabulary.excerpt: If someone had a clear Nazi tattoo on them, you probably could consume or conclude that there's a Nazi sympathizer.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他被指控是极端主义同情者。

vocabulary.en: research conducted to find damaging information about political opponents
vocabulary.excerpt: Is that what oppo research -- like, isn't that what you're supposed to do?
vocabulary.example_cn: 竞选团队进行了深入的对手研究。

vocabulary.en: a piece of paper on which you mark your vote
vocabulary.excerpt: They are still counting ballots in California.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们仍在清点加利福尼亚州的选票。

vocabulary.en: an election in which voters choose the candidates from each party who will compete in the main election
vocabulary.excerpt: the primary race for governor remains too close to call.
vocabulary.example_cn: 州长初选仍然难分胜负。

vocabulary.en: the person who currently holds a particular official position
vocabulary.excerpt: incumbent Democrat Karen Bass will advance to a November runoff.
vocabulary.example_cn: 现任民主党人凯伦·巴斯将进入11月的决选。

vocabulary.en: a second election or vote to decide a winner when the first one is not conclusive
vocabulary.excerpt: incumbent Democrat Karen Bass will advance to a November runoff.
vocabulary.example_cn: 现任民主党人凯伦·巴斯将进入11月的决选。

vocabulary.en: to admit that you have lost a game, election, etc.
vocabulary.excerpt: Trump-backed Randy Feenstra conceding to businessman Zach Lahn.
vocabulary.example_cn: 特朗普支持的兰迪·芬斯特拉承认败给商人扎克·拉恩。

vocabulary.en: a public statement of support for a person, product, etc.
vocabulary.excerpt: not because of the president's endorsement.
vocabulary.example_cn: 并非因为总统的支持。

vocabulary.en: with no opponent or competitor
vocabulary.excerpt: Kean ran unopposed.
vocabulary.example_cn: 基恩在无对手的情况下参选。

vocabulary.en: the act of officially suggesting someone for a position or award
vocabulary.excerpt: Deb Haaland won the Democratic nomination for governor.
vocabulary.example_cn: 德布·哈兰德赢得了民主党州长提名。

vocabulary.en: relating to or including people of many different cultures
vocabulary.excerpt: We're a multicultural state.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我们是一个多元文化的州。

vocabulary.en: the act of representing someone or something
vocabulary.excerpt: I think representation matters.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我认为代表很重要。

vocabulary.en: a forecast or estimate of a future situation
vocabulary.excerpt: Why aren't we making any projections at this point?
vocabulary.example_cn: 为什么我们现在不做任何预测？

vocabulary.en: to stamp a mark on a letter or package showing the date and place of posting
vocabulary.excerpt: if your mail ballot is postmarked by Election Day yesterday
vocabulary.example_cn: 如果你的邮寄选票在昨天选举日之前盖有邮戳

vocabulary.en: impossible to imagine or think of
vocabulary.excerpt: It's not completely inconceivable that he catches either Becerra or Steve Hilton.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他赶上贝塞拉或史蒂夫·希尔顿并非完全不可能。

vocabulary.en: the process of changing the boundaries of electoral districts
vocabulary.excerpt: California was also involved in this big redistricting fight.
vocabulary.example_cn: 加利福尼亚州也卷入了这场重大的选区重划之争。

vocabulary.cn: 丛林初选（所有候选人同台，前两名晋级）
vocabulary.en: a primary election in which all candidates, regardless of party, appear on the same ballot and the top two advance
vocabulary.excerpt: in this so-called jungle primary where the top two candidates will advance to November.
vocabulary.example_cn: 在这场所谓的丛林初选中，前两名候选人将进入11月大选。

vocabulary.en: something that is different from what is usual or expected
vocabulary.excerpt: speaking of anomalies
vocabulary.example_cn: 说到异常情况

vocabulary.en: not supporting or controlled by any political party
vocabulary.excerpt: this is a nonpartisan race technically
vocabulary.example_cn: 严格来说这是一场无党派竞选。

vocabulary.en: Political Action Committee, an organization that raises money to support or oppose political candidates
vocabulary.excerpt: outside groups in PAC spent $79 million
vocabulary.example_cn: 外部团体和政治行动委员会花费了7900万美元。

vocabulary.excerpt: it was one of the proxy conversations of the night
vocabulary.example_cn: 这是当晚的代理对话之一。

vocabulary.en: in favor of social reform or new ideas
vocabulary.excerpt: how would progressive candidates do?
vocabulary.example_cn: 进步派候选人表现如何？

vocabulary.en: having opinions that are not extreme
vocabulary.excerpt: progressives versus moderates
vocabulary.example_cn: 进步派与温和派

vocabulary.en: the people in positions of power in a society or organization
vocabulary.excerpt: not necessarily backed by the establishments
vocabulary.example_cn: 不一定得到当权派的支持

vocabulary.en: the basic systems and services that a country or organization needs to function
vocabulary.excerpt: local infrastructure matters
vocabulary.example_cn: 地方基础设施很重要。

vocabulary.en: a disagreement about something important
vocabulary.excerpt: There was a lot of controversy over his past
vocabulary.example_cn: 他的过去存在很大争议。

vocabulary.en: to choose only the best or most suitable from a group
vocabulary.excerpt: which part of the resume you wanted to cherry pick
vocabulary.example_cn: 你想挑选简历的哪一部分

vocabulary.en: the idea that someone is guilty because of their connection with a guilty person
vocabulary.excerpt: just because I am Arab does not mean I'm guilt by association
vocabulary.example_cn: 仅仅因为我是阿拉伯人并不意味着我因关联而有罪。

vocabulary.en: long-lasting military conflicts that seem to have no end
vocabulary.excerpt: anti-forever wars argument
vocabulary.example_cn: 反永久战争的论点

vocabulary.cn: 民粹主义的；民粹主义者
vocabulary.en: relating to a political approach that claims to represent ordinary people
vocabulary.excerpt: the populist activists within the party
vocabulary.example_cn: 党内的民粹主义活动家

vocabulary.en: to give public support to someone or something
vocabulary.excerpt: the president endorsed our lieutenant governor
vocabulary.example_cn: 总统支持我们的副州长。

vocabulary.en: very thin or weak
vocabulary.excerpt: who has a paper-thin resume
vocabulary.example_cn: 他的简历非常单薄。

vocabulary.en: a feeling or impression that someone or something gives
vocabulary.excerpt: the vibration of that endorsement
vocabulary.example_cn: 那次支持的反响

sentences.en: If there is one trend we've generally seen in the years as the Republicans tend to fall as we get more of the votes in from California, and Democrats tend to rise, and I think that was part of the reason why Steve Helton was saying in that clip that you played, hey, can I keep that position over Tom Steyer remaining in 3rd place?
sentences.cn: 如果说有一个我们多年来普遍看到的趋势，那就是随着加州更多选票计入，共和党人的得票率往往会下降，而民主党人则会上升，我认为这就是史蒂夫·希尔顿在你们播放的片段中所说的部分原因：嘿，我能保持领先于汤姆·斯泰尔、让他继续排在第三的位置吗？
sentences.structure: If there is one trend (that) we've generally seen in the years as the Republicans tend to fall as we get more of the votes in from California, and Democrats tend to rise, and I think that was part of the reason why Steve Helton was saying in that clip that you played, hey, can I keep that position over Tom Steyer remaining in 3rd place?
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，包含条件状语从句（If there is one trend...）、定语从句（we've generally seen...）、时间状语从句（as we get more...）、并列句（and I think...）以及宾语从句（why Steve Helton was saying...）。其中"as"引导时间状语从句，表示"随着"；"why"引导宾语从句，解释原因。

sentences.en: If Tom Steyer can actually start turning some of this Columbia blue in a Navy blue for him, hey, then it might be game on.
sentences.cn: 如果汤姆·斯泰尔真的能开始把一些哥伦比亚蓝变成海军蓝（指选票颜色变化），那么比赛可能就开始了。
sentences.structure: If Tom Steyer can actually start turning some of this Columbia blue in a Navy blue for him, hey, then it might be game on.
sentences.analysis: 这是一个条件状语从句，主句为"it might be game on"。"turning...in..."是动名词短语作宾语，"Columbia blue"和"Navy blue"是比喻，分别代表不同候选人的支持区域。

sentences.en: The question is, will the trend that we see, that we expect to see, that is the later ballots being more Democratic, can they in fact allow her to close that gap with Pratt?
sentences.cn: 问题是，我们看到的、我们预期看到的趋势——即后来的选票更倾向于民主党——是否真的能让她缩小与普拉特的差距？
sentences.structure: The question is, will the trend (that we see, that we expect to see, that is the later ballots being more Democratic) can they in fact allow her to close that gap with Pratt?
sentences.analysis: 这是一个主系表结构，表语是一个疑问句。疑问句中主语"the trend"后跟三个并列的定语从句（that we see, that we expect to see, that is...），其中第三个定语从句中"being more Democratic"是现在分词短语作定语修饰"ballots"。

sentences.en: And I think the question is, where can those remaining votes, that 44 percent that we still have left out, where can Tom Steyer make up that ground?
sentences.cn: 我认为问题是，那些剩余的选票，即我们尚未统计的44%，汤姆·斯泰尔能在哪里弥补差距？
sentences.structure: And I think the question is, where can those remaining votes, (that 44 percent that we still have left out), where can Tom Steyer make up that ground?
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句为"I think"，后接宾语从句。宾语从句中"the question is"后跟一个疑问句，其中"that 44 percent"是"those remaining votes"的同位语，"that we still have left out"是定语从句修饰"44 percent"。

sentences.en: And I think that was part of the reason why Steve Helton was saying in that clip that you played, hey, can I keep that position over Tom Steyer remaining in 3rd place?
sentences.cn: 我认为这就是史蒂夫·希尔顿在你们播放的片段中所说的部分原因：嘿，我能保持领先于汤姆·斯泰尔、让他继续排在第三的位置吗？
sentences.structure: And I think that was part of the reason why Steve Helton was saying in that clip that you played, hey, can I keep that position over Tom Steyer remaining in 3rd place?
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句为"I think"，后接宾语从句。宾语从句中"that was part of the reason"后跟"why"引导的定语从句，其中"that you played"是定语从句修饰"clip"，最后直接引语"hey, can I..."是"saying"的内容。

sentences.en: And I think the question is, where can those remaining votes, that 44 percent that we still have left out, where can Tom Steyer make up that ground?
sentences.cn: 我认为问题是，那些剩余的选票，即我们尚未统计的44%，汤姆·斯泰尔能在哪里弥补差距？
sentences.structure: And I think the question is, where can those remaining votes, (that 44 percent that we still have left out), where can Tom Steyer make up that ground?
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句为"I think"，后接宾语从句。宾语从句中"the question is"后跟一个疑问句，其中"that 44 percent"是"those remaining votes"的同位语，"that we still have left out"是定语从句修饰"44 percent"。

sentences.en: The latest projections show Republican Steven Hilton and Democrat Xavier Becerra in the top two positions with billionaire Tom Steyer in third.
sentences.cn: 最新预测显示共和党人史蒂文·希尔顿和民主党人泽维尔·贝塞拉位居前两名，亿万富翁汤姆·斯泰尔位列第三。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾结构：主语（The latest projections）+ 谓语（show）+ 宾语（Republican Steven Hilton and Democrat Xavier Becerra）+ 宾语补足语（in the top two positions）+ 伴随状语（with billionaire Tom Steyer in third）。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个简单句，谓语show后接复合宾语，其中in the top two positions是宾语补足语，with结构作伴随状语，表示并列情况。

sentences.en: As long as we can make it into the top two, Californians will be able to change the trajectory of our state away from policies that have given us the highest poverty rate, the highest unemployment rate in the country, the highest cost of living -- all the problems that we know about.
sentences.cn: 只要我们能够进入前两名，加州人就能改变我们州的发展轨迹，摆脱那些给我们带来全国最高贫困率、最高失业率、最高生活成本的政策——所有我们熟知的问题。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（As long as we can make it into the top two）+ 主句（Californians will be able to change the trajectory of our state away from policies）+ 定语从句（that have given us...）。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，As long as引导条件状语从句，主句中away from policies是介词短语作状语，policies后接that引导的定语从句，定语从句中并列了三个名词短语作宾语。

sentences.en: Historically speaking, the late ballots have tended to shift the overall vote to be more Democratic.
sentences.cn: 从历史上看，晚到的选票往往使整体投票结果更倾向于民主党。
sentences.structure: 插入语（Historically speaking）+ 主谓宾结构：主语（the late ballots）+ 谓语（have tended to shift）+ 宾语（the overall vote）+ 宾语补足语（to be more Democratic）。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个简单句，Historically speaking是独立成分，谓语tend to后接不定式短语shift...to be...，其中to be more Democratic是宾语补足语。

sentences.en: And what's so interesting is look at the margin.
sentences.cn: 而非常有趣的是看看这个差距。
sentences.structure: 主语从句（What's so interesting）+ 系动词（is）+ 表语（look at the margin）。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个主系表结构，主语由what引导的主语从句充当，表语是省略to的不定式短语（祈使语气）。

sentences.en: I think back to the fact that Trump -- the first time he ran he lost the Iowa caucuses, and it turns out he lost in Iowa yet again here in the Republican primary or caucus.
sentences.cn: 我回想起一个事实：特朗普第一次竞选时输掉了爱荷华州党团会议，而事实证明他在这次共和党初选或党团会议中再次在爱荷华州失利。
sentences.structure: 主句（I think back to the fact）+ 同位语从句（that Trump...lost...and it turns out...）。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，fact后接that引导的同位语从句，从句由两个并列分句组成，第二个分句中it turns out后接省略that的宾语从句。

sentences.en: And what is so important to note here is while Kean ran unopposed, this is a district that only voted for Donald Trump I believe by about one point -- one point in that district.
sentences.cn: 这里需要特别注意的是，虽然基恩没有对手，但这个选区据我看来只以大约一个百分点的优势投票给了唐纳德·特朗普——在该选区仅一个百分点。
sentences.structure: 主语从句（What is so important to note here）+ 系动词（is）+ 表语从句（while Kean ran unopposed, this is a district...）。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个主系表结构，主语由what引导的主语从句充当，表语是一个复合句，包含while引导的让步状语从句和主句，主句中district后接that引导的定语从句，I believe是插入语。

sentences.en: And so what we're dealing with now is an absentee congressman who has not been seen for a very long period of time.
sentences.cn: 所以我们现在面对的是一个长期不见踪影的缺席议员。
sentences.structure: 主语从句（what we're dealing with now）+ 系动词（is）+ 表语（an absentee congressman）+ 定语从句（who has not been seen...）。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个主系表结构，主语由what引导的主语从句充当，表语congressman后接who引导的定语从句。

sentences.en: You can remember last year in New Jersey, in that gubernatorial race, the Democrat easily, easily, easily won.
sentences.cn: 你还记得去年在新泽西州长竞选中，民主党人轻松、轻松、轻松地获胜。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾结构：主语（You）+ 谓语（can remember）+ 宾语（last year in New Jersey, in that gubernatorial race, the Democrat easily, easily, easily won）。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个简单句，宾语是一个省略了that的从句，从句中主语the Democrat，谓语won，easily重复三次表示强调。

sentences.en: And the question is if New Jersey is as blue this time around and given that this is a swing district, can Tom Kean actually survive this general election come November?
sentences.cn: 问题是，如果新泽西这次仍然那么蓝，而且鉴于这是一个摇摆选区，汤姆·基恩真的能在11月的大选中幸存吗？
sentences.structure: 主句（the question is）+ 表语从句（if New Jersey is as blue...and given that...can Tom Kean actually survive...）。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个主系表结构，表语从句是一个复合句，包含if引导的条件状语从句和given that引导的状语，主句是疑问句can Tom Kean actually survive...。

sentences.en: I think that at the end of the day, like, this race will end up being when you look at the Democrats that ascended and how Becerra sort of benefited from Swalwell's fall and from the allegations and him having to pull out of the race, part of it is because of just the experience, the resume.
sentences.cn: 我认为归根结底，当你审视那些晋升的民主党人以及贝塞拉如何从斯瓦尔韦尔的落马、指控以及他被迫退出竞选中受益时，部分原因就在于经验和履历。
sentences.structure: 主句（I think）+ 宾语从句（that...this race will end up being...part of it is because of...）。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，宾语从句由that引导，从句中嵌套了when引导的时间状语从句，主句是this race will end up being...，后面part of it is because of...是并列分句。

sentences.en: And so what we're dealing with now is an absentee congressman who has not been seen for a very long period of time.
sentences.cn: 所以我们现在面对的是一个长期不见踪影的缺席议员。
sentences.structure: 主语从句（what we're dealing with now）+ 系动词（is）+ 表语（an absentee congressman）+ 定语从句（who has not been seen...）。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个主系表结构，主语由what引导的主语从句充当，表语congressman后接who引导的定语从句。

sentences.en: I think that at the end of the day, like, this race will end up being when you look at the Democrats that ascended and how Becerra sort of benefited from Swalwell's fall and from the allegations and him having to pull out of the race, part of it is because of just the experience, the resume.
sentences.cn: 我认为归根结底，当你审视那些晋升的民主党人以及贝塞拉如何从斯瓦尔韦尔的落马、指控以及他被迫退出竞选中受益时，部分原因就在于经验和履历。
sentences.structure: 主句（I think）+ 宾语从句（that...this race will end up being...part of it is because of...）。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，宾语从句由that引导，从句中嵌套了when引导的时间状语从句，主句是this race will end up being...，后面part of it is because of...是并列分句。

sentences.en: Tap into anger about a lot of issues that Angelinos have about affordability, quality of life issues.
sentences.cn: 利用洛杉矶居民对可负担性和生活质量等诸多问题的不满。
sentences.structure: 祈使句，动词短语"tap into"作谓语，"anger"作宾语，后接介词短语"about..."，其中"that"引导定语从句修饰"issues"。
sentences.analysis: 该句为祈使句，主语省略。"tap into"是固定短语，意为“利用、开发”。"that"引导的定语从句修饰先行词"issues"，从句中"Angelinos have"是主谓结构，"about"引出具体方面。

sentences.en: The latest projections show Republican Steve Hilton and Democrat Xavier Becerra at the head of the pack.
sentences.cn: 最新预测显示共和党人史蒂夫·希尔顿和民主党人泽维尔·贝塞拉处于领先位置。
sentences.structure: 简单句，主语"projections"，谓语"show"，宾语"Steve Hilton and Xavier Becerra"，介词短语"at the head of the pack"作宾语补足语。
sentences.analysis: 该句结构清晰。"projections"是名词，意为“预测”。"at the head of the pack"是习语，意为“处于领先地位”。

sentences.en: We're going to build a city where parents and kids do not have to navigate tents, because in the nation's second largest city, there should never be anybody that is sleeping on our streets.
sentences.cn: 我们将建设一个父母和孩子不必穿行帐篷的城市，因为在这个全国第二大城市，绝不应该有人睡在街头。
sentences.structure: 复合句，主句"We're going to build a city"，后接"where"引导的定语从句修饰"city"，"because"引导原因状语从句，其中包含"that"引导的定语从句修饰"anybody"。
sentences.analysis: 该句包含多层从句。"where"引导定语从句，在从句中作地点状语。"navigate"意为“穿越、应对”。"because"从句中"there should never be"是存在句的否定形式。

sentences.en: I think she tried to make it a generational race, and we're starting to see that across the Democratic ecosystem in these primaries.
sentences.cn: 我认为她试图将其变成一场代际竞选，我们开始在这些初选中看到这一点贯穿民主党生态系统。
sentences.structure: 并列复合句，第一分句"I think"后接省略"that"的宾语从句，第二分句"we're starting to see"后接"that"引导的宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: 第一分句中"make it a generational race"是“make+宾语+宾语补足语”结构。第二分句中"that"指代前文提到的现象。"across the Democratic ecosystem"是介词短语作状语。

sentences.en: What we've seen is that Spencer Pratt, apparently, is actually appealing to real voters and their concerns.
sentences.cn: 我们看到的是，斯宾塞·普拉特显然确实在吸引真实选民及其关切。
sentences.structure: 主系表结构，主语"What we've seen"是主语从句，系动词"is"，表语为"that"引导的表语从句。
sentences.analysis: 主语从句"What we've seen"在句中作主语。表语从句中"apparently"是插入语，"appealing to"是动词短语，意为“吸引”。

sentences.en: The question has been whether she's going to face a city councilwoman or a former reality TV star.
sentences.cn: 问题一直是她将面对一位市议员还是一位前真人秀明星。
sentences.structure: 主系表结构，主语"The question"，系动词"has been"，表语为"whether"引导的表语从句。
sentences.analysis: "whether...or..."引导表语从句，表示选择。"face"意为“面对”，后接两个并列宾语。

sentences.en: I think the question now will be, if he does become officially, you know, going into a runoff with Mayor Bass, is of course, him actually articulating what his plans are.
sentences.cn: 我认为现在的问题是，如果他确实正式进入与巴斯市长的决选，当然，他能否真正阐明自己的计划。
sentences.structure: 复合句，主句"I think"后接宾语从句，宾语从句中主语"the question"，系动词"will be"，表语为"him actually articulating..."，其中包含"if"引导的条件状语从句和"what"引导的宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: 该句口语化特征明显。"if"从句中"does"表示强调。"you know"是插入语。"him actually articulating"是动名词复合结构作表语。"what his plans are"是宾语从句。

sentences.en: Because once you're an incumbent, and you're in the most powerful position within the positions, you have to run as an outsider in order to challenge, quote unquote, "the status quo."
sentences.cn: 因为一旦你是现任者，处于职位中最有权力的位置，你就必须作为局外人参选，以便挑战所谓的“现状”。
sentences.structure: 复合句，"Because"引导原因状语从句，从句中包含"once"引导的时间状语从句，主句为"you have to run..."。
sentences.analysis: "once"引导时间状语从句，意为“一旦”。"quote unquote"是口语表达，意为“所谓的”。"in order to"表示目的。

sentences.en: The other aspect, I think she tried to make it a generational race, and we're starting to see that across the Democratic ecosystem in these primaries.
sentences.cn: 另一方面，我认为她试图将其变成一场代际竞选，我们开始在这些初选中看到这一点贯穿民主党生态系统。
sentences.structure: 并列复合句，第一分句"I think"后接宾语从句，第二分句"we're starting to see"后接"that"引导的宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: 该句与前面句子结构类似。"the other aspect"是名词短语作状语。"make it a generational race"是“make+宾语+宾语补足语”结构。

sentences.en: What I'm paying attention to, though, Audie, and my team is, is we're analyzing the married vote in California.
sentences.cn: 不过，奥迪，我和我的团队关注的是，我们正在分析加州的已婚选民投票情况。
sentences.structure: 主系表结构，主语"What I'm paying attention to"是主语从句，系动词"is"，表语为"we're analyzing..."从句。
sentences.analysis: 主语从句"What I'm paying attention to"在句中作主语。"though"是副词，意为“不过”。"Audie"是呼语。表语从句说明具体内容。

sentences.en: As long as we can make it into the top two, Californians will be able to change the trajectory of our state away from policies that have given us the highest poverty rate, the highest unemployment rate in the country, the highest cost of living, all the problems that we know about, but we can turn them around with common sense, practical ideas such as the ones I'm running on to make our state, in my word, Califordable.
sentences.cn: 只要我们能够进入前两名，加州人就能改变我们州的轨迹，摆脱那些给我们带来全国最高贫困率、最高失业率、最高生活成本的政策，所有我们知道的问题，但我们可以用常识和实用的想法来扭转局面，比如我竞选时提出的那些，让我们的州，用我的话来说，变得‘加州可负担’。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（As long as...）+ 主句（Californians will be able to...）+ 定语从句（that have given us...）+ 并列结构（all the problems...）+ 转折（but we can turn them around...）+ 同位语（such as the ones...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，包含条件状语从句、定语从句和并列结构。主句是Californians will be able to change the trajectory of our state away from policies，后面跟了一个定语从句修饰policies。然后列举了三个最高级短语，接着用but转折，最后用such as举例说明。

sentences.en: Why aren't we making any projections at this point? It's because of this number down here. Just 57 percent at this point of the vote has been counted. An estimated 57 percent has been counted. Why? Because in California, where there's a lot of mail vote, if your mail ballot is postmarked by Election Day yesterday, it can be counted for the next 13 days, so we don't know at this point how many more ballots will be mailed in.
sentences.cn: 为什么我们现在不做任何预测？是因为这里的这个数字。目前只有57%的选票被统计了。估计有57%已被统计。为什么？因为在加州，邮寄选票很多，如果你的邮寄选票在昨天选举日之前盖有邮戳，它可以在接下来的13天内被统计，所以我们目前不知道还会有多少选票被寄来。
sentences.structure: 设问句（Why...?）+ 回答（It's because of...）+ 解释（Because in California, where...）+ 条件状语从句（if your mail ballot...）+ 结果（so we don't know...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个设问句，先提出问题，然后给出原因。原因部分包含一个非限制性定语从句（where there's a lot of mail vote）修饰California，以及一个条件状语从句（if your mail ballot is postmarked...）说明邮寄选票的规则，最后用so引导结果。

sentences.en: And one thing we do know about California and this mail vote, as it comes in later, it does tend to get more Democratic, bluer.
sentences.cn: 关于加州和邮寄选票，我们确实知道一件事，随着选票后来陆续到来，它们确实倾向于更民主党、更蓝。
sentences.structure: 主语（one thing）+ 定语从句（we do know about...）+ 时间状语从句（as it comes in later）+ 主句（it does tend to get...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句是one thing...it does tend to get...，其中包含一个定语从句修饰one thing，以及一个时间状语从句as it comes in later。

sentences.en: So if it ended right now, these would be the top two to advance and the Democrat Richard Pan would be shut out.
sentences.cn: 所以如果现在结束，这两位将是晋级的前两名，而民主党人理查德·潘将被排除在外。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（if it ended right now）+ 主句（these would be...and the Democrat...would be shut out）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个虚拟条件句，表示与现在事实相反的假设。主句由两个并列分句组成，用and连接。

sentences.en: And I think one of the weaknesses of these self-funding billionaires is that they think they're really, really smart.
sentences.cn: 我认为这些自筹资金的亿万富翁的弱点之一是他们认为自己非常非常聪明。
sentences.structure: 主句（I think）+ 宾语从句（one of the weaknesses...is that...）+ 表语从句（that they think...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句是I think，后面跟了一个宾语从句，其中包含一个表语从句that they think they're really, really smart。

sentences.en: And I think there's a certain segment of the Democratic base who respects that.
sentences.cn: 我认为有一部分民主党基础选民尊重这一点。
sentences.structure: 主句（I think）+ 宾语从句（there's a certain segment...who respects that）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句是I think，后面跟了一个宾语从句，其中包含一个定语从句who respects that修饰segment。

sentences.en: And I think that's going to be an interesting dynamic coming out of this is clearly him having the resume.
sentences.cn: 我认为这将是一个有趣的动态，从中可以看出他显然拥有这样的履历。
sentences.structure: 主句（I think）+ 宾语从句（that's going to be...）+ 同位语（coming out of this is clearly him having the resume）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句是I think，后面跟了一个宾语从句，其中包含一个同位语结构解释dynamic。

sentences.en: And I think that the president -- I mean, if we're honest, sometimes he does like a last minute endorsement when things are clearly already headed in a direction.
sentences.cn: 我认为总统——我的意思是，老实说，有时他确实喜欢在事情已经明显朝着某个方向发展时进行最后一刻的支持。
sentences.structure: 主句（I think）+ 宾语从句（that the president...does like...）+ 插入语（I mean, if we're honest）+ 时间状语从句（when things are clearly already headed in a direction）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句是I think，后面跟了一个宾语从句，其中包含插入语和时间状语从句。

sentences.en: And I think that is the difference we're starting to see in some of these primaries and I think that's going to be the measurement in South Carolina and certainly the case in Iowa.
sentences.cn: 我认为这就是我们开始在一些初选中看到的差异，我认为这将成为南卡罗来纳州的衡量标准，当然也是爱荷华州的情况。
sentences.structure: 并列句（I think...and I think...），每个分句包含宾语从句
sentences.analysis: 这是一个并列复合句，由and连接两个分句，每个分句都是I think引导的宾语从句。

sentences.en: And by the way, a three judge panel, two Trump judges ruled that this was the discriminatory map not too long ago.
sentences.cn: 顺便说一句，一个由三名法官组成的小组，其中两名是特朗普任命的法官，不久前裁定这是一张歧视性的地图。
sentences.structure: 插入语（by the way）+ 主语（a three judge panel）+ 同位语（two Trump judges）+ 谓语（ruled）+ 宾语从句（that this was the discriminatory map）+ 时间状语（not too long ago）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个简单句，包含插入语和同位语，谓语ruled后跟宾语从句。

topics.title: California Jungle Primary
topics.content: 加州采用‘丛林初选’（jungle primary）制度，所有候选人无论党派同台竞争，得票前两名进入大选，无论党派。2026年加州州长初选竞争激烈，共和党人史蒂夫·希尔顿和民主党人泽维尔·贝塞拉领先，亿万富翁汤姆·斯泰尔第三。由于加州邮寄选票计票较慢，后期选票通常更倾向民主党，因此结果仍有变数。
topics.keywords: jungle primary, California governor race, Steve Hilton, Xavier Becerra, Tom Steyer

topics.title: Los Angeles Mayor Race
topics.content: 洛杉矶市长初选采用无党派丛林初选，现任市长凯伦·巴斯（民主党）领先，前真人秀明星斯宾塞·普拉特（共和党倾向）第二，市议员妮提亚·拉曼（民主党进步派）第三。普拉特因在帕利塞兹大火中批评政府而获得关注，其名人效应和AI视频策略引发讨论。巴斯希望与普拉特对决，因为民调显示她轻松获胜；若拉曼晋级，则竞争更激烈。
topics.keywords: Los Angeles mayor, Karen Bass, Spencer Pratt, Nithya Raman, runoff

topics.title: Trump's Anti-Weaponization Fund
topics.content: 特朗普政府设立的近18亿美元‘反武器化基金’旨在赔偿被政府‘武器化’对待的人，但遭到国会共和党人强烈反对，代理司法部长托德·布兰奇在众议院小组委员会作证时宣布放弃该基金。然而，特朗普及其家族和企业的税收豁免等政策仍在推进。
topics.keywords: anti-weaponization fund, Todd Blanche, congressional backlash, tax immunity

topics.title: New Jersey Detention Center Controversy
topics.content: 新泽西州德莱尼霍尔移民拘留设施因被指控条件不人道而引发抗议，州政府起诉私人公司以获取健康检查权限。
topics.keywords: Delaney Hall, immigrant detention, inhumane conditions, lawsuit

topics.title: U.S. Military Action Against Oil Tanker
topics.content: 美国中央司令部称用地狱火导弹击中一艘悬挂博茨瓦纳国旗的油轮，该油轮无视警告驶向伊朗哈尔克岛。
topics.keywords: hellfire missile, oil tanker, Iran, U.S. Central Command

topics.title: California Governor Primary Election
topics.content: 2026年6月2日，加利福尼亚州举行州长初选。由于众议员埃里克·斯瓦尔韦尔因不当行为指控退出，竞争变得激烈。最新预测显示共和党人史蒂夫·希尔顿和民主党人泽维尔·贝塞拉位居前两名，亿万富翁汤姆·斯泰尔位列第三。前两名将进入11月的大选。加州采用“丛林初选”制度，所有候选人同台竞争，得票最高的两人进入大选，无论党派。
topics.keywords: California, governor, primary, runoff, Steve Hilton, Xavier Becerra, Tom Steyer

topics.title: Los Angeles Mayor's Race
topics.content: 洛杉矶市长选举中，现任民主党人凯伦·巴斯将进入11月的决胜选举。共和党真人秀明星斯宾塞·普拉特排名第二，很可能成为她的对手。普拉特利用选民对 affordability 和生活质量问题的愤怒，但能否提出具体计划仍是疑问。
topics.keywords: Los Angeles, mayor, runoff, Karen Bass, Spencer Pratt, affordability

topics.title: Iowa Republican Gubernatorial Primary
topics.content: 爱荷华州共和党州长初选中，特朗普支持的候选人兰迪·芬斯特拉输给了商人扎克·拉恩。拉恩是农民和商人，从未担任公职，但得到了“让美国再次健康”运动的支持。这打破了特朗普在共和党初选中一贯的胜选记录。
topics.keywords: Iowa, governor, primary, Trump endorsement, Randy Feenstra, Zach Lahn, MAHA

topics.title: New Mexico Democratic Gubernatorial Primary
topics.content: 新墨西哥州前内政部长德布·哈兰德以超过70%的得票率赢得民主党州长提名。如果当选，她将成为该州第一位美洲原住民州长。哈兰德强调代表性在政治中的重要性。
topics.keywords: New Mexico, governor, primary, Deb Haaland, Native American, representation

topics.title: Progressive vs. Establishment Dynamics
topics.content: 本次初选夜显示，进步派候选人表现不佳。例如，加州参议员凯蒂·波特仅获4.6%的选票，洛杉矶市长候选人尼提亚·拉曼也未能进入决胜轮。而建制派候选人如贝塞拉和巴斯则表现更好。这反映了民主党内部进步派与建制派之间的张力。
topics.keywords: progressive, establishment, Democratic Party, Katie Porter, Nithya Raman

topics.title: Veterans in Politics
topics.content: 退伍军人团体“Vote Vet”在本次选举中投入970万美元支持爱荷华州参议员候选人乔什·图雷克。该团体利用反战情绪，强调退伍军人问题。图雷克以“厌倦了为亿万富翁服务的体制”为口号，试图吸引跨党派选民。
topics.keywords: veterans, Vote Vet, Josh Turek, anti-war, Iowa Senate

topics.title: California Primary Elections
topics.content: California's primary elections use a 'jungle primary' system where all candidates, regardless of party, appear on the same ballot. The top two vote-getters advance to the general election, regardless of party affiliation. This system often leads to unusual matchups, such as a Democrat vs. a Republican or two Democrats. The 2026 California governor's race features Republican Steve Hilton and Democrat Xavier Becerra as frontrunners, with billionaire Tom Steyer close behind. The L.A. mayor's race shows incumbent Democrat Karen Bass advancing, likely facing Republican reality TV star Spencer Pratt or Democrat Nithya Raman.
topics.keywords: California, primary, jungle primary, governor, mayor, runoff

topics.title: Los Angeles Mayor's Race
topics.content: The Los Angeles mayoral race is nonpartisan in the primary, but candidates' party affiliations are known. Incumbent Karen Bass (D) is projected to advance to the November runoff. Her likely opponent is Spencer Pratt (R), a former reality TV star known from 'The Hills,' who has gained traction by focusing on homelessness and affordability. City councilwoman Nithya Raman (D) is also in contention, representing a progressive alternative. Key issues include homelessness, housing affordability, and quality of life. Bass faces significant opposition, with over 50% voting against her in the primary.
topics.keywords: Los Angeles, mayor, Karen Bass, Spencer Pratt, Nithya Raman, homelessness, affordability

topics.title: California Governor's Race
topics.content: The 2026 California governor's race is highly competitive. Republican Steve Hilton, a former adviser to David Cameron, and Democrat Xavier Becerra, former U.S. Attorney General, are leading. Billionaire Tom Steyer (D) is close behind. The race has been chaotic, with high-profile Democrats like Kamala Harris and Alex Padilla opting not to run. No endorsements from Governor Newsom or Nancy Pelosi. The outcome could have national implications, potentially serving as a referendum on President Trump.
topics.keywords: California, governor, Steve Hilton, Xavier Becerra, Tom Steyer, election

topics.title: Rise of Outsider Candidates
topics.content: The 2026 primaries highlight the rise of outsider candidates, particularly in the Republican Party. Spencer Pratt, a reality TV star with no political experience, secured over 30% of the vote in the L.A. mayor's race. This mirrors the Trump phenomenon, where celebrity status and anti-establishment messaging resonate with voters. Similarly, Steve Hilton, though a former political adviser, positions himself as a disruptor. The trend raises questions about the role of experience and the power of name recognition in modern politics.
topics.keywords: outsider, candidate, reality TV, disruptor, anti-establishment

topics.title: Homelessness and Affordability in California
topics.content: Homelessness and housing affordability are central issues in California elections. In Los Angeles, voters express frustration with tent encampments and high living costs. Spencer Pratt's focus on homelessness resonates with some voters, including a former homeless person who credits Pratt's proposed services. Incumbent Karen Bass emphasizes her record on reducing homelessness. The broader affordability crisis affects families, artists, and workers, driving support for candidates who promise change.
topics.keywords: homelessness, affordability, housing, Los Angeles, California

topics.title: Media and Politics: Scott Pelley Firing
topics.content: Veteran journalist Scott Pelley was fired from '60 Minutes' after accusing CBS news chief Bari Weiss of 'murdering the news program.' Pelley criticized the collapse of values at CBS and doubled down on his remarks post-firing. This incident reflects ongoing tensions in news media over editorial direction and journalistic integrity, especially under new leadership.
topics.keywords: Scott Pelley, 60 Minutes, CBS, Bari Weiss, journalism, firing

topics.title: Senate Candidate Controversies
topics.content: Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner faces scandals including a Nazi tattoo and alleged sexual text messages. Despite Democratic leaders like Chuck Schumer expressing support, the controversies raise concerns about his electability in a key race to unseat Senator Susan Collins. The situation highlights the challenges parties face when candidates have damaging personal histories.
topics.keywords: Graham Platner, Maine, Senate, scandal, Nazi tattoo, electability

topics.title: California Primary Election 2026
topics.content: 2026年加州初选，包括州长、洛杉矶市长和国会选区选举。州长初选中，共和党人史蒂夫·希尔顿和民主党人泽维尔·贝塞拉领先，亿万富翁汤姆·斯泰尔第三。由于加州邮寄选票制度，计票可能持续13天，结果尚未确定。洛杉矶市长初选中，现任市长凯伦·巴斯和真人秀明星斯宾塞·普拉特领先。
topics.keywords: California primary, governor race, jungle primary, mail-in ballot, Tom Steyer, Karen Bass

topics.title: Trump Endorsement and Its Impact
topics.content: 特朗普的支持在初选中的影响力。在爱荷华州州长初选中，特朗普支持的兰迪·芬斯特拉输给了扎克·拉恩，表明特朗普的支持并非万能。在新泽西州，特朗普支持的汤姆·基恩因无对手而获胜。分析认为，特朗普的“支持”和“背书”有区别，后者包括积极竞选活动。
topics.keywords: Trump endorsement, primary election, Iowa, New Jersey, Randy Feenstra, Zach Lahn

topics.title: Progressive vs. Moderate Democrats
topics.content: 民主党内部进步派与温和派的竞争。在加州，贝塞拉作为温和派表现良好，而斯泰尔作为进步派亿万富翁未能获得广泛支持。在旧金山，州参议员斯科特·维纳击败了佩洛西支持的候选人。分析认为，选民更看重经验和稳定性。
topics.keywords: progressive, moderate, Democratic Party, Xavier Becerra, Tom Steyer, Nancy Pelosi

topics.title: Military Veterans in Politics
topics.content: 有军事背景的候选人成为趋势。在蒙大拿州，退伍军人阿拉尼·班克黑德赢得参议院初选。分析认为，民主党试图通过招募退伍军人来增强其“爱国”形象，以对抗共和党。同时，反永久战争情绪也在影响选举。
topics.keywords: military veteran, Vote Vets, anti-forever wars, Alani Bankhead, Montana

topics.title: Redistricting and Voting Rights
topics.content: 加州选区重划和阿拉巴马州最高法院关于歧视性地图的裁决。在加州第6选区，独立候选人凯文·基利领先，民主党人理查德·潘可能被排除。在阿拉巴马州，最高法院允许实施可能削弱黑人投票权的地图，引发争议。
topics.keywords: redistricting, voting rights, Alabama, Supreme Court, racial discrimination, California 6th district
